tool_args.label: Refinancing of ESD TIFIA Loan for Moynihan Station Development Project Phase 2
tool_args.raw_text: refinancing of an existing loan (the "Existing TIFIA Loan") up to an amount not to exceed $633 million between New York State Urban Development Corporation d/b/a Empire State Development ("ESD") and the United States Department of Transportation

payload.project_name: Refinancing of ESD TIFIA Loan for Moynihan Station Development Project Phase 2
payload.description: To obtain MTA Board approval to enter into various agreements necessary to support the refinancing of an existing TIFIA loan up to $633 million for Phase 2 of the Moynihan Station Development Project, which included a new Train Hall shared by Amtrak, LIRR and Metro-North.

evidence_refs.source_quote: To obtain MTA Board approval to enter into various agreements necessary to support the refinancing of an existing loan (the 'Existing TIFIA Loan') up to an amount not to exceed $633 million between New York State Urban Development Corporation d/b/a Empire State Development ('ESD') and the United States Department of Transportation

In 2017, MTA Board approved the execution and delivery of, among other things, (1) a Joint Services Agreement...to effectuate Phase 2 of the Moynihan Station Development Project (the 'Project'), which included a new Train Hall shared by Amtrak, Long Island Rail Road and Metro-North Commuter Railroad.

tool_args.label: MTA PeopleSoft ERP System Upgrade
tool_args.raw_text: amendment to competitively solicited, miscellaneous service contract with Bias Corporation to support upgrading MTA's PeopleSoft ERP System

payload.description: Upgrade to MTA's PeopleSoft ERP System which includes upgrade to People data, digital transformation, and functional enhancements. Part of the MTA Transformation Plan approved by the MTA Board in June 2019.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Board approval is sought to amend a competitively solicited, miscellaneous service contract with Bias Corporation to support upgrading MTA's PeopleSoft ERP System which includes upgrade to People data, digital transformation, and functional enhancements, in the amount not-to-exceed (NTE) $11.7M. This is a critical part of the MTA Transformation Plan which was approved by the MTA Board in June 2019
